what_it_does: Defines a systemd service unit that launches the Company Kokoro TTS Python service on port 4123, sets environment overrides to disable the Hugging Face Xet backend and force CPU ONNX execution, and integrates the service into company.target with restart-on-failure behavior.
observations: Service is Type=simple and runs /home/tim/company/.voice-venv/bin/python voice/tts_service.py 4123
Sets HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 to avoid HF Xet backend stalls on this network
Pins ONNX_PROVIDER=CPUExecutionProvider to avoid a WSL2 CUDA provider device-discovery hang
StandardOutput/StandardError go to the journal with SyslogIdentifier company-tts-kokoro
Comments explain the operational rationale for each environment workaround
standouts: Environment workarounds for network and WSL2/CUDA hangs are explicitly documented in comments
CPU-only ONNX execution for a voice synth that requires no VRAM
Bound to company.target via both PartOf and WantedBy
conventions: Follows systemd unit section ordering: Unit, Service, Install
Uses absolute paths for WorkingDirectory and ExecStart
Uses Environment= lines for runtime configuration
concerns: Hard-coded user home path /home/tim/company limits portability
Port 4123 is embedded directly in ExecStart rather than parameterized
No explicit User= or Group= in the Service section
Restart=on-failure could mask repeated startup failures
notes: This is the systemd wrapper for the Kokoro TTS service, carrying two documented environment workarounds for network and WSL2 GPU-discovery issues.
questions: Is the service intended to run as user tim or a dedicated service account?
How is port 4123 allocated and documented elsewhere?
What other units are included in company.target and where is it defined?
Is voice/tts_service.py the only TTS entrypoint or are there sibling services?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (Company TTS — Kokoro voice service on :4123) matches the ExecStart command and port; the environment overrides are additional operational mitigations not implied by the Description.
apparent_themes: Integration into a larger company service target
intention_signals: The author intends to provide a reliable, automatically restarted TTS endpoint that avoids known environmental failure modes (HF Xet stalls and WSL2 CUDA hangs) by pinning CPU inference and tying the service into a company-wide systemd target.
novelty: Inline operational rationale tying environment variables to specific runtime failure modes (HF Xet stalls, WSL2 CUDA hang).
contribution: Captures the exact runtime environment, launch command, port, restart policy, and documented workarounds needed to run the Kokoro TTS service under systemd as part of company.target.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd service unit that starts a Kokoro TTS Python service on port 4123 from a virtual environment, with environment overrides disabling HF Xet and forcing CPU ONNX execution, and integrates into company.target.
intention_for_embedding: To make the Kokoro TTS service start reliably under systemd while avoiding network and WSL2 GPU-discovery stalls.
